content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM APL environment for COR24. Single-panel REPL with printer-style emulation, floating hardware panel (S2 switch, D2 LED, UART TX/RX), demo dropdown, file upload, and reset. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator path dep. Phase 2: REPL panel (scrolling output, 6-space indent prompt, keyboard capture) + control bar. Phase 3: emulator integration (EmulatorBridge, UART I/O bridge, batch execution, pre-assemble APL interpreter via build.rs). Phase 4: hardware panel + demo registry + file upload. Phase 5: polish, responsive design, GitHub Pages deployment. Phase 6: APL glyph prettification display modes. Phase 7: New demos showcasing interpreter features (bracket indexing, bitwise ops, reverse/catenate, take/drop, error handling, control flow loops). Phase 8: Extend prettification to cover new keywords (rev, cat, and, or, not, goto). Phase 9: Command history with up/down arrow keys. Phase 10: Scroll-lock behavior (auto-scroll pauses when user scrolls up). Phase 11: APL quick-reference help overlay. Phase 12: Deploy all new features to GitHub Pages.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-project [production]: Scaffold the Yew/Rust/WASM project for web-sw-cor24-apl. Follow the exact pat...
  [x] 002-repl-panel [legacy]: Create the REPL panel component. Follow the patterns from web-sw-cor24-pcode'...
  [x] 003-003-emulator-integration [production]: Wire the COR24 emulator into the REPL panel so that APL input is evaluated by...
  [x] 004-004-control-bar-hardware-panel [production]: Add control bar (Reset button, demo dropdown, file upload) and floating hardw...
  [x] 005-005-polish-responsive-deploy [production]: Phase 5: Polish, responsive design, and GitHub Pages deployment.

1. Responsi...
  [x] 006-006-apl-glyph-prettification [production]: Phase 6: APL glyph prettification display modes.

1. Create a prettification ...
  [ ] 007-007-new-demos [production]: Phase 7: Add new demo programs showcasing interpreter features not yet demoed... <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 008-008-extend-prettify [production]: Phase 8: Extend APL glyph prettification to cover keywords added in recent in...
  [ ] 009-009-command-history [production]: Phase 9: Add command history with up/down arrow key support to the REPL.

In ...
  [ ] 010-010-scroll-lock [production]: Phase 10: Implement scroll-lock behavior for the REPL output area.

Currently...
  [ ] 011-011-help-overlay [production]: Phase 11: Add an APL quick-reference help overlay.

Create a toggleable help ...
  [ ] 012-012-deploy-phase7-11 [production]: Phase 12: Build, test, and deploy all Phase 7-11 features to GitHub Pages.

1...

=== CURRENT STEP: 007-007-new-demos [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Phase 7: Add new demo programs showcasing interpreter features not yet demoed...
Task type: feature

=== PROMPT ===
Phase 7: Add new demo programs showcasing interpreter features not yet demoed in the web UI.

Add the following demos to src/demos.rs, inserting them in logical order among existing demos:

1. **Reverse & Catenate** -- 'rev 1 2 3 4 5', 'cat 2 3 rho iota 6' (ravel), '1 2 3 cat 4 5 6' (catenate)
2. **Take & Drop** -- 'A <- iota 10', '3 take A', '_3 take A' (negative = from end), '2 drop A', '_2 drop A', '2 take 2 3 rho iota 6' (matrix take)
3. **Bracket Indexing** -- 'V <- 10 20 30 40 50', 'V[0]', 'V[3]', 'V[2] <- 99', 'V'
4. **Bitwise Operations** -- '7 and 3', '5 or 3', 'not 0', '1 2 3 and 3 2 1', practical example: 'MMIO qsvo 242', 'MMIO[257] and 2'
5. **Error Handling** -- showcase all 6 error types: SYNTAX ERROR (@bad), DOMAIN ERROR (1/0), VALUE ERROR (UNDEF), LENGTH ERROR (1 2 3+4 5), RANK ERROR (+/2 3 rho iota 6), then show REPL recovers
6. **Control Flow** -- a counting loop: 'I <- 5', 'LOOP:', '[] <- I', 'I <- I - 1', 'goto (I)/LOOP', '[] <- 0' to show goto/label/conditional-branch

Ensure all demos compile and run correctly on the interpreter. Keep the Demo struct (name, description, source) pattern. Put Hardware I/O and Shared Variables last since they are hardware-specific.

=== PAST SUCCESSES (feature) ===
  [2026-03-30T20:35:57] action=created control_bar.rs, hardware.rs, demos.rs; refactored lib.rs to Component; extended repl.rs with Properties and hardware tracking; added CSS for control bar and floating hardware panel; added web-sys features for FileReader/Select, result=success, reward=+1

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

file.content:
//! Demo registry — APL programs fed as text via UART to the interpreter.

pub struct Demo {
    pub name: &'static str,
    pub description: &'static str,
    pub source: &'static str,
}

/// Demo programs in COR24 APL ASCII syntax.
/// Each line is sent to the interpreter via UART as if the user typed it.
pub const DEMOS: &[Demo] = &[
    Demo {
        name: "Hardware I/O",
        description: "LED and switch via quad-variables",
        source: "\
qled
qled <- 1
qled
qled <- 0
qled
qsw
qled <- qsw
qled
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Shared Variables",
        description: "MMIO via qsvo 242 (AP 242 shared variable coupling)",
        source: "\
MMIO qsvo 242
MMIO[0]
MMIO[0] <- 1
MMIO[0]
MMIO[257]
MMIO[0] <- 0
MMIO[0]
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Iota & Reduce",
        description: "Index generation and reduction",
        source: "\
iota 5
iota 10
+/ 1 2 3 4 5
+/ iota 10
-/ 1 2 3
*/ 1 2 3 4
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Matrices",
        description: "Matrix operations with reshape",
        source: "\
2 3 rho iota 6
(2 3 rho iota 6) + (2 3 rho 10 20 30 40 50 60)
10 + 2 3 rho iota 6
(2 3 rho iota 6) * 2
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Reshape",
        description: "Rho for reshape and shape-of",
        source: "\
3 rho 1 2 3
5 rho 1 2 3
2 3 rho 1 2 3 4 5 6
rho 2 3 rho iota 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Scalars",
        description: "Scalar arithmetic and parentheses",
        source: "\
3 + 4
10 - 3
6 * 4
20 / 5
2 + 3 * 4
(2 + 3) * 4
_3 + 10
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Variables",
        description: "Assignment and variable use",
        source: "\
A <- 5
A + 3
B <- A * 2
B
A + B
)VARS
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Vectors",
        description: "Vector literals and element-wise operations",
        source: "\
1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10 20 30
2 * 1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10
rho 1 2 3 4 5
",
    },
];

content.text: Now I'll add the 6 new demos and move Hardware I/O and Shared Variables to the end.

content: demos are to be listed in alphabetical order, please
a tutorial (future) could introduce concepts in an easy-to-hard order (future)

input.new_string:
/// Demo programs in COR24 APL ASCII syntax (alphabetical order).
/// Each line is sent to the interpreter via UART as if the user typed it.
pub const DEMOS: &[Demo] = &[
    Demo {
        name: "Bitwise Operations",
        description: "Bitwise and, or, not on integers",
        source: "\
7 and 3
5 or 3
not 0
not 1
1 2 3 and 3 2 1
MMIO qsvo 242
MMIO[257] and 2
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Bracket Indexing",
        description: "Read and write vector elements by index",
        source: "\
V <- 10 20 30 40 50
V[0]
V[3]
V[2] <- 99
V
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Control Flow",
        description: "Counting loop with goto and labels",
        source: "\
I <- 5
LOOP:
[] <- I
I <- I - 1
goto (I)/LOOP
[] <- 0
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Error Handling",
        description: "All six error types, REPL recovers",
        source: "\
@bad
1 / 0
UNDEF
1 2 3 + 4 5
+/ 2 3 rho iota 6
3 + 4
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Hardware I/O",
        description: "LED and switch via quad-variables",
        source: "\
qled
qled <- 1
qled
qled <- 0
qled
qsw
qled <- qsw
qled
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Iota & Reduce",
        description: "Index generation and reduction",
        source: "\
iota 5
iota 10
+/ 1 2 3 4 5
+/ iota 10
-/ 1 2 3
*/ 1 2 3 4
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Matrices",
        description: "Matrix operations with reshape",
        source: "\
2 3 rho iota 6
(2 3 rho iota 6) + (2 3 rho 10 20 30 40 50 60)
10 + 2 3 rho iota 6
(2 3 rho iota 6) * 2
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Reshape",
        description: "Rho for reshape and shape-of",
        source: "\
3 rho 1 2 3
5 rho 1 2 3
2 3 rho 1 2 3 4 5 6
rho 2 3 rho iota 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Reverse & Catenate",
        description: "Reverse, ravel, and catenate arrays",
        source: "\
rev 1 2 3 4 5
cat 2 3 rho iota 6
1 2 3 cat 4 5 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Scalars",
        description: "Scalar arithmetic and parentheses",
        source: "\
3 + 4
10 - 3
6 * 4
20 / 5
2 + 3 * 4
(2 + 3) * 4
_3 + 10
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Shared Variables",
        description: "MMIO via qsvo 242 (AP 242 shared variable coupling)",
        source: "\
MMIO qsvo 242
MMIO[0]
MMIO[0] <- 1
MMIO[0]
MMIO[257]
MMIO[0] <- 0
MMIO[0]
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Take & Drop",
        description: "Select elements from the front or end",
        source: "\
A <- iota 10
3 take A
_3 take A
2 drop A
_2 drop A
2 take 2 3 rho iota 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Variables",
        description: "Assignment and variable use",
        source: "\
A <- 5
A + 3
B <- A * 2
B
A + B
)VARS
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Vectors",
        description: "Vector literals and element-wise operations",
        source: "\
1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10 20 30
2 * 1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10
rho 1 2 3 4 5
",
    },
];

(2 3 rho iota 6) * 2
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Reshape",
        description: "Rho for reshape and shape-of",
        source: "\
3 rho 1 2 3
5 rho 1 2 3
2 3 rho 1 2 3 4 5 6
rho 2 3 rho iota 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Reverse & Catenate",
        description: "Reverse, ravel, and catenate arrays",
        source: "\
rev 1 2 3 4 5
cat 2 3 rho iota 6
1 2 3 cat 4 5 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Scalars",
        description: "Scalar arithmetic and parentheses",
        source: "\
3 + 4
10 - 3
6 * 4
20 / 5
2 + 3 * 4
(2 + 3) * 4
_3 + 10
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Shared Variables",
        description: "MMIO via qsvo 242 (AP 242 shared variable coupling)",
        source: "\
MMIO qsvo 242
MMIO[0]
MMIO[0] <- 1
MMIO[0]
MMIO[257]
MMIO[0] <- 0
MMIO[0]
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Take & Drop",
        description: "Select elements from the front or end",
        source: "\
A <- iota 10
3 take A
_3 take A
2 drop A
_2 drop A
2 take 2 3 rho iota 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Variables",
        description: "Assignment and variable use",
        source: "\
A <- 5
A + 3
B <- A * 2
B
A + B
)VARS
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Vectors",
        description: "Vector literals and element-wise operations",
        source: "\
1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10 20 30
2 * 1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10
rho 1 2 3 4 5
",
    },
];
toolUseResult.originalFile:
//! Demo registry — APL programs fed as text via UART to the interpreter.

pub struct Demo {
    pub name: &'static str,
    pub description: &'static str,
    pub source: &'static str,
}

/// Demo programs in COR24 APL ASCII syntax.
/// Each line is sent to the interpreter via UART as if the user typed it.
pub const DEMOS: &[Demo] = &[
    Demo {
        name: "Hardware I/O",
        description: "LED and switch via quad-variables",
        source: "\
qled
qled <- 1
qled
qled <- 0
qled
qsw
qled <- qsw
qled
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Shared Variables",
        description: "MMIO via qsvo 242 (AP 242 shared variable coupling)",
        source: "\
MMIO qsvo 242
MMIO[0]
MMIO[0] <- 1
MMIO[0]
MMIO[257]
MMIO[0] <- 0
MMIO[0]
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Iota & Reduce",
        description: "Index generation and reduction",
        source: "\
iota 5
iota 10
+/ 1 2 3 4 5
+/ iota 10
-/ 1 2 3
*/ 1 2 3 4
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Matrices",
        description: "Matrix operations with reshape",
        source: "\
2 3 rho iota 6
(2 3 rho iota 6) + (2 3 rho 10 20 30 40 50 60)
10 + 2 3 rho iota 6
(2 3 rho iota 6) * 2
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Reshape",
        description: "Rho for reshape and shape-of",
        source: "\
3 rho 1 2 3
5 rho 1 2 3
2 3 rho 1 2 3 4 5 6
rho 2 3 rho iota 6
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Scalars",
        description: "Scalar arithmetic and parentheses",
        source: "\
3 + 4
10 - 3
6 * 4
20 / 5
2 + 3 * 4
(2 + 3) * 4
_3 + 10
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Variables",
        description: "Assignment and variable use",
        source: "\
A <- 5
A + 3
B <- A * 2
B
A + B
)VARS
",
    },
    Demo {
        name: "Vectors",
        description: "Vector literals and element-wise operations",
        source: "\
1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10 20 30
2 * 1 2 3
1 2 3 + 10
rho 1 2 3 4 5
",
    },
];

content.text:
# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```

---

## Usage
```
/mw-cp
/mw-cp adds OAuth token refresh with retry logic

feat(demos): add 6 new demos, reorder all alphabetically

Add demos for Bitwise Operations, Bracket Indexing, Control Flow
(goto/labels), Error Handling (all 6 error types), Reverse & Catenate,
and Take & Drop. Reorder all 14 demos alphabetically by name so the
dropdown is predictable and scannable.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
